identity.name: Taverners Hill Makers District
identity.description: A mixed-use redevelopment proposal along Parramatta Road that repositions a traditional industrial strip into a creative production and makers precinct.

opportunity.title: Strategic Parramatta Road location
opportunity.description: The site sits within the Parramatta Road corridor between Taverners Hill Light Rail Station, Lewisham Train Station and Norton Street, positioning it within a major urban renewal context.

opportunity.title: Large industrial landholding
opportunity.description: The site provides a rare large-scale landholding within a constrained Inner West precinct, creating an opportunity for density, employment evolution and public domain improvement.

opportunity.title: Creative ecosystem extension
opportunity.description: The project offers an opportunity to extend the Inner West creative ecosystem eastward through artisan studios, galleries, cultural spaces and creative industry tenancies.

challenges.title: Employment land transition
challenges.description: The project needed to support housing growth while retaining the employment character of the land and avoiding a generic residential redevelopment outcome.

challenges.title: Parramatta Road renewal
challenges.description: The proposal needed to contribute positively to Parramatta Road through activation, connectivity, walkability and improved public domain.

challenges.title: Human scale within high density
challenges.description: The proposal needed to introduce substantial height and density while maintaining a human-scaled ground plane and local Inner West character.

designResponse.title: Creative makers district
designResponse.description: The proposal reframes the site from a traditional industrial strip into a creative production hub where art, design, craftsmanship and cultural enterprise can thrive.

designResponse.title: Public domain network
designResponse.description: The proposal introduces a laneway network, footpath widening and a public plaza to improve permeability and create new public benefit.

designResponse.title: Mixed-use employment model
designResponse.description: Retail, food, commercial and creative industry tenancies are combined with residential density to preserve employment character while supporting housing growth.

designResponse.title: Green urbanity
designResponse.description: The project leverages proximity to the GreenWay and surrounding open spaces to support liveability, walkability and higher density living near green infrastructure.

outcome.summary: The report concludes that the proposal has significant urban design merit and recommends that it be supported.
benefits: New public domain and plaza spaces
Retention and evolution of employment character
Support for creative industry and makers uses
Contribution to Inner West housing and renewal objectives

whyItMatters: This project demonstrates Hatch's ability to reposition industrial land into a high-density mixed-use precinct while retaining employment character, supporting creative industries and improving public domain outcomes along a major urban corridor.
lessonsLearned: Large inner-city industrial sites near transit can support higher density renewal without abandoning employment character.
A makers district narrative can make mixed-use density feel place-specific rather than generic.
Public domain improvements are central to making high-density corridor renewal acceptable and valuable.

placeFramework.name: Seven Essential Elements of Great Places

studioAssets.shortSummary: Taverners Hill Makers District transforms a large Parramatta Road industrial site into a mixed-use creative production precinct, combining housing, employment, public domain and makers uses within a strategic Inner West renewal corridor.
studioAssets.capabilityStatement: Hatch provided urban design analysis, strategic positioning and place-based design principles for Taverners Hill Makers District, a planning proposal that repositions industrial land into a high-density mixed-use creative precinct while retaining employment character and improving public domain outcomes.
studioAssets.tenderParagraph: This project demonstrates experience in complex urban renewal, employment land transition, transit-oriented density, place strategy and public domain-led planning proposals.

searchQuestionsThisProjectShouldAnswer: Show me projects about employment land transition.
Find mixed-use projects along major corridors.
Find projects where public domain helped justify density.
Show projects involving creative industries or makers precincts.
Find projects connected to the Parramatta Road corridor.

story.brief: Provide urban design analysis, strategic positioning and design principles to support a mixed-use redevelopment and planning proposal for a large site along Parramatta Road in Leichhardt.
story.challenge: The project needed to balance housing growth, employment land retention, industrial character, transport access, public domain improvement and Inner West local identity within a complex Parramatta Road corridor context.
story.response: The proposal reframes the site from a traditional industrial strip into a creative production hub, combining housing, employment, retail, food, commercial and creative industry uses with new public domain, laneways, widened footpaths and public plazas.
story.outcome: The report concludes that the proposal has significant urban design merit by enhancing activation, connectivity, walkability and mixed-use outcomes for Leichhardt.
story.lessons: Large inner-city industrial sites near transit and green infrastructure can support higher density renewal while retaining employment character, creative production, public benefit and human-scaled public domain.
